raw.title: How environmental destruction is built into corporate design
raw.excerpt: Modern corporations are legally and financially structured to prioritize profit over ecological stability. The result is a system that normalizes environmental destruction while diffusing responsibility across institutions and individuals.

llm_processed.summary: The design of corporate systems often prioritizes profit over environmental sustainability, leading to environmental destruction.
llm_processed.lateral_potential: This could enable a deeper understanding of how corporate systems harm the environment and how to redesign them for sustainability.
